subject: Mathematics
title: Thales of Miletus
description: A short lesson to help you apply Thales of Miletus.

hook.text: Around 600 BC, a Greek merchant did something no one had done—he proved a math statement logically.

buildup.text: Thales didn't just observe that base angles in an isosceles triangle are equal. He proved it.

discovery.text: He's credited with five geometry theorems, making him the first known mathematician in history.

twist.text: He also predicted a solar eclipse, used geometry to measure the height of pyramids, and fell into a well while stargazing.

climax.text: Thales showed that nature follows rules that reason can discover—the birth of science.

punchline.text: One man's logic launched 2,600 years of mathematics.

quiz.question: What was Thales' key contribution to mathematics?
